./docs/spec-build/epics/01-claude-impl-cli-skill/artifacts/epic-verification/005-synthesizer-codex-gpt54-xhigh.jsonl:133:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_74","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba src/phases/claude-impl.md | sed -n '1,220p'"","aggregated_output":"     1	# Claude Implementation
     2	
     3	Use `ls-claude-impl` when a completed spec pack already exists and Claude Code needs a disciplined implementation workflow with a bundled bounded-operation CLI.
     4	
     5	## Startup Orientation
     6	
     7	Resolve the spec-pack root before reading deeply. The minimum v1 layout is:
     8	
     9	- `epic.md`
    10	- `tech-design.md`
    11	- `test-plan.md`
    12	- `stories/`
    13	
    14	Run `bin/ls-impl-cli.cjs inspect --spec-pack-root <path> --json` as the first runtime check. Capture the returned tech-design shape, required artifacts, story inventory, and insert status before you move on to run configuration or provider-backed work.
    15	
    16	Read every story file in `stories/` in order before starting Story 0 or Story 1. Do not begin implementation from a partial story read.
    17	
    18	## Initialization Steps
    19	
    20	1. Confirm the spec-pack root and run `inspect`.
    21	2. If `team-impl-log.md` is absent, create `team-impl-log.md` and initialize the run in `SETUP`.
    22	3. If `team-impl-log.md` already exists, read it first and resume from the recorded state.
    23	4. Treat `team-impl-log.md`, `impl-run.config.json`, and the CLI result artifacts under `artifacts/` as the recovery surface for a resumed run.
    24	5. Treat missing prior chat or tool-call context as a normal recovery case, not a blocker.
    25	6. Record whether the spec pack is a two-file or four-file tech-design layout.
    26	7. Record whether `custom-story-impl-prompt-insert.md` and `custom-story-verifier-prompt-insert.md` are present. Their absence is non-blocking.
    27	8. Discover verification gates in this precedence order: explicit CLI flags, `impl-run.config.json` entries if the run uses them later, package scripts, project policy docs, then CI configuration.
    28	9. If the policy stays ambiguous after that precedence order, pause for a user decision instead of improvising.
    29	10. Run `preflight` before provider-backed work.
    30	11. After `preflight`, record the validated run configuration, available providers, active role defaults, verification gates, and degraded fallback conditions in `team-impl-log.md`.
    31	
    32	## Default Resolution Contract
    33	
    34	- Claude Code is the primary harness for every run.
    35	- If Codex CLI is available, default the story implementor to Codex `gpt-5.4` with high reasoning.
    36	- If Codex CLI is available, default the verifier pair to Codex `gpt-5.4` with extra high reasoning plus Claude Sonnet with high reasoning.
    37	- If Codex CLI is available, default epic verifier GPT lanes and the epic synthesizer to Codex `gpt-5.4` with extra high reasoning, paired with a Claude Sonnet verifier lane.
    38	- If Codex is unavailable but Copilot CLI is available, use Copilot for the fresh-session GPT roles and keep the retained story implementor on Claude Sonnet high.
    39	- If no GPT-capable secondary harness is available, switch the run to Claude-only defaults, record the degraded-diversity condition explicitly, and note that verifier diversity is weaker in this run.
    40	
    41	## State Ownership
    42	
    43	- `impl-run.config.json` is orchestrator-owned configuration.
    44	- `team-impl-log.md` is the durable run record.
    45	- The CLI performs one bounded operation per call and returns structured results.
    46	- The CLI does not own story progression, acceptance, or recovery strategy between calls.
    47	- Run the final story gate yourself after implementation evidence and verifier results are complete.
    48	- compile deferred and accepted-risk items into a durable cleanup artifact before epic verification begins.
    49	- Run the final epic gate yourself after cleanup, epic verification, and synthesis are complete.
    50	- Compare the current total test count to the prior accepted baseline before accepting a story.
    51	- Advance to the next story only from the committed codebase, the ordered story list, `team-impl-log.md`, and the recorded artifacts on disk.
    52	
    53	## Read Next
    54	
    55	After the initial `inspect` pass and log setup, read:
    56	
    57	1. `references/claude-impl-reading-journey.md`
    58	2. `references/claude-impl-process-playbook.md`
    59	3. `references/claude-impl-cli-operations.md`
    60	4. `references/claude-impl-prompt-system.md`

./docs/spec-build/epics/01-claude-impl-cli-skill/artifacts/epic-verification/005-synthesizer-codex-gpt54-xhigh.jsonl:146:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_81","type":"agent_message","text":"{"resultId":"es-2026-04-21T16:30:00Z-codex-gpt54-xhigh-01","outcome":"needs-fixes","confirmedIssues":["Verification gate discovery only searches inside the spec-pack root for `package.json`, `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, and `.github/workflows`, even though the skill/process contract says to look at repo-root package scripts and repository policy docs. I reproduced this on a valid nested spec pack inside this repo with no local policy files: `preflight` returned `needs-user-decision` instead of discovering the repo's `green-verify` / `verify-all` gates, so AC-1.6 is not met for nested packs.","Provider availability handling does not implement the documented `auth-unknown` tier for Codex/Copilot. `provider-checks.ts` always runs `<provider> auth status`, marks the harness unavailable when that command fails, and `preflight` blocks with `PROVIDER_UNAVAILABLE`; I reproduced this with a fake Codex binary that supports `--version` but not `auth status`, which blocked the run instead of surfacing a usable binary-present/auth-unknown state.","Story implementor and story verifier prompt assembly widens the bounded reading journey by always inserting the epic path when `epicPath` is present. `story-implementor.ts` and `story-verifier.ts` always pass `epicPath`, and `prompt-assembly.ts` always emits `- Epic: ...`, even though the epic and skill/process contracts bound those roles to the current story, the full tech-design set, and the test plan.","The `preflight` invalid-run-config path violates the CLI artifact-persistence contract. When `impl-run.config.json` is malformed, `preflight` emits a blocked JSON envelope with `artifacts: []` and writes no `artifacts/preflight/*.json` file, even though the runtime design says the persisted artifact must mirror the stdout envelope so recovery does not depend on stdout capture."],"disputedOrUnconfirmedIssues":["The claim that package-script gate discovery must record the canonical commands `bun run green-verify` / `bun run verify-all` rather than the script bodies remains unresolved. The design pack's canonical-mapping examples prefer the shorter commands, but the implementation and `gate-discovery.test.ts` intentionally return the actual script bodies, so the evidence is internally inconsistent.","The hardcoded four-file companion allowlist is not a confirmed current-epic defect. One runtime-design section describes a generic `tech-design-*.md` rule, but the design index, current epic artifacts, and runtime tests consistently use exactly `tech-design-cli-runtime.md` and `tech-design-skill-process.md`, so I could not show broken current behavior from today's evidence.","The visible `smoke:impl-cli` skip and the missing `processes/impl-cli/tests/packaged-cli-smoke.test.ts` filename are not confirmed closeout defects. The design explicitly allows a visible skip in the earliest version, `bun run verify-all` still passes, and `scripts/__tests__/claude-impl-build.test.ts` already exercises the bundled CLI under Node.","The non-strict `impl-run.config.json` schema, `epic-cleanup`'s reuse of `quick_fixer` role settings, the broad `error.message.includes(\"config\")` checks, and the parallel-gate test interference are implementation smells, but I did not find epic or tech-design language that makes them current contract violations.","The `epic-cleanup` `/\\bAPPROVED\\b/i` matcher could plausibly misread negated prose, but the cleanup-batch format is not specified tightly enough for me to prove a present contract failure from the current evidence.","The verifier-batch `block` versus generic routing-matrix `blocked` terminology is not a confirmed bug. Verifier operations are explicitly specified as `pass` / `revise` / `block`, and the envelope status still maps blocked verifier runs to `status: \"blocked\"` with exit code 3."],"readinessAssessment":"`bun run verify-all` passes in the current workspace with 247 passing tests and the expected visible `smoke:impl-cli skipped` notice, so the implementation is broadly integrated. Even so, I confirmed two blocking runtime contract misses: nested spec packs cannot auto-discover repo-root verification gates, and provider availability hard-blocks Codex/Copilot when `auth status` is unsupported instead of using the designed `auth-unknown` tier. I also confirmed two non-blocking drifts: story-role reading journeys include the epic path beyond the bounded scope, and `preflight` does not persist its blocked envelope when run-config parsing fails. The remaining verifier-raised items were either explicitly allowed by the design, speculative, or depended on contradictory design/test artifacts that I could not resolve into a clean defect. Because the blocking issues are concrete and reproducible, this epic is not ready for closeout yet; it needs targeted fixes rather than another verification round.","recommendedNextStep":"Route a focused fix pass on `processes/impl-cli/core/gate-discovery.ts`, `processes/impl-cli/core/provider-checks.ts`, `processes/impl-cli/commands/preflight.ts`, and `processes/impl-cli/core/prompt-assembly.ts`, add regression tests for nested spec-pack gate discovery, auth-unsupported provider handling, bounded story-role reading journeys, and malformed-config artifact persistence, then rerun `bun run verify-all` before asking for a fresh closeout verification/synthesis pass."}"}}

item.text:
I’ve finished the reading journey and mapped the exact code paths. Plan from here:

1. Add the red tests for the runtime items first:
   - nested spec-pack repo-root gate discovery
   - provider-availability tiers and preflight non-blocking behavior
   - bounded story-role reading journeys
   - malformed-config artifact persistence
   - cleanup approval regex edge cases
   - flexible `tech-design-*.md` companion discovery
   - prompt-insert guard for non-story roles
   - strict config parsing and typed config-load classification
   - unknown-outcome fallback coverage

2. Run a targeted red test slice and confirm each failure is for the intended contract gap.

3. Implement only the scoped fixes, then update the five docs/spec alignment items.

4. Rebaseline the test guard for the intentional S3 gate-discovery expectation shift, run `bun run green-verify`, and return the per-item disposition cleanly.

I’ve got enough context to start the red phase. I’m editing the test files now to add only the missing coverage for these 15 items, including the intentional canonical-gate expectation shift where the current tests pin behavior that contradicts the design.
The first red patch hit a context mismatch in `result-contracts.test.ts`, so I’m splitting the test edits into smaller patches to keep the diff precise and avoid collateral churn.
The red cases are in place now. I’m running a focused test slice against the touched files to make sure the failures line up with the intended contract gaps before I start implementation.

100	The CLI with no arguments should print quick-start help only. The full process explanation stays in the skill docs.
   101	
   102	### Command Signatures
   103	
   104	The public signatures should stay stable even if internal implementation changes.
   105	
   106	| Command | Signature |
   107	|---|---|
   108	| `inspect` | `ls-impl-cli inspect --spec-pack-root <path> [--json]` |
   109	| `preflight` | `ls-impl-cli preflight --spec-pack-root <path> [--config <path>] [--story-gate <cmd>] [--epic-gate <cmd>] [--json]` |
   110	| `story-implement` | `ls-impl-cli story-implement --spec-pack-root <path> --story-id <id> [--config <path>] [--json]` |
   111	| `story-continue` | `ls-impl-cli story-continue --spec-pack-root <path> --story-id <id> --provider <provider> --session-id <id> [--config <path>] [--followup-file <path> | --followup-text <text>] [--json]` |
   112	| `story-verify` | `ls-impl-cli story-verify --spec-pack-root <path> --story-id <id> [--config <path>] [--json]` |
   113	| `quick-fix` | `ls-impl-cli quick-fix --spec-pack-root <path> (--request-file <path> | --request-text <text>) [--config <path>] [--json]` |
   114	| `epic-cleanup` | `ls-impl-cli epic-cleanup --spec-pack-root <path> --cleanup-batch <path> [--config <path>] [--json]` |
   115	| `epic-verify` | `ls-impl-cli epic-verify --spec-pack-root <path> [--config <path>] [--json]` |
   116	| `epic-synthesize` | `ls-impl-cli epic-synthesize --spec-pack-root <path> --verifier-report <path> --verifier-report <path> [--config <path>] [--json]` |
   117	
   118	All commands should support `--json` for machine-readable output. Human-readable summaries may exist for manual use, but the orchestrator contract should be the JSON payload.
   119	
   120	### Public CLI IO Contract
   121	
   122	The CLI should behave consistently across commands.
   123	
   124	| Surface | Rule |
   125	|---|---|
   126	| stdout with `--json` | emit exactly one `CliResultEnvelope<T>` JSON object |
   127	| stdout without `--json` | emit a concise human-readable summary only |
   128	| stderr | reserved for progress/debugging; never required for orchestration routing |
   129	| persisted artifacts | write the same JSON envelope that was emitted on stdout |
   130	| default config path | `<spec-pack-root>/impl-run.config.json` unless `--config` overrides it |
   131	| default artifact root | `<spec-pack-root>/artifacts/` |
   132	
   133	### Command Examples
   134	
   135	```bash
   136	node bin/ls-impl-cli.cjs inspect --spec-pack-root /abs/spec-pack --json
   137	node bin/ls-impl-cli.cjs preflight --spec-pack-root /abs/spec-pack --json
   138	node bin/ls-impl-cli.cjs story-implement --spec-pack-root /abs/spec-pack --story-id story-03 --json
   139	node bin/ls-impl-cli.cjs story-verify --spec-pack-root /abs/spec-pack --story-id story-03 --json
   140	node bin/ls-impl-cli.cjs epic-cleanup --spec-pack-root /abs/spec-pack --cleanup-batch /abs/spec-pack/artifacts/cleanup/cleanup-batch.md --json
   141	```
   142	
   143	For commands whose inner payload also contains an `outcome`, the envelope `outcome` is the routing source of truth and must match the inner payload exactly. Schema validation should reject mismatches.
   144	
   145	### Exit Codes
   146	
   147	Exit codes are a coarse routing signal. The JSON payload remains the source of truth.
   148	
   149	| Exit Code | Meaning |
   150	|---|---|
   185	│   ├── preflight.ts
   186	│   ├── story-implement.ts
   187	│   ├── story-continue.ts

   500	
   501	1. Read markdown files from `stories/`, excluding `coverage.md`.
   502	2. Derive `storyId` from the filename without extension.
   503	3. Order stories by the leading numeric prefix in the filename when present.
   504	4. If two files have the same numeric prefix, fall back to lexical filename order.
   505	5. If no numeric prefixes exist anywhere, use lexical filename order and emit a warning in `inspect`.
   506	6. If the naming pattern is mixed and produces ambiguous ordering, return `needs-user-decision`.
   507	
   508	This keeps story order reproducible and makes the ordering rules explicit for both runtime code and tests.
   509	
   510	Examples:
   511	
   512	- `00-foundation.md`, `01-run-setup.md`, `02-prompts.md` -> valid, ordered numerically
   513	- `01-a.md`, `01-b.md` -> valid, ordered lexically within the same prefix
   514	- `01-a.md`, `b.md`, `02-c.md` -> ambiguous mixed pattern, return `needs-user-decision`
   515	
   516	### Tech Design Companion Discovery Rules
   517	
   518	The four-file tech-design shape is valid only when:
   519	
   520	- `tech-design.md` exists
   521	- `test-plan.md` exists
   522	- exactly two additional files matching `tech-design-*.md` exist
   523	- `tech-design.md` itself is excluded from the companion count
   524	- companion paths are sorted lexically before being returned
   525	
   526	Invalid examples:
   527	
   528	- one companion file only
   529	- three or more companion files
   530	- unrelated files such as `tech-design-notes.md` or `tech-design-archive.md` inside the spec-pack root
   531	
   532	These should return `blocked` with an explicit shape error rather than being ignored silently.
   533	
   534	### TC Mapping for This Flow
   535	
   536	| TC | Tests | Module | Setup | Assert |
   537	|---|---|---|---|---|
   538	| TC-1.1a | accepts two-file config | `inspect-command.test.ts` | fixture with `epic.md`, `tech-design.md`, `test-plan.md`, `stories/` | `inspect` returns `ready` and `two-file` |
   539	| TC-1.1b | accepts four-file config | `inspect-command.test.ts` | fixture with companions | `inspect` returns `ready` and `four-file` |
   540	| TC-1.1c | rejects `stories.md` layout | `inspect-command.test.ts` | invalid story layout fixture | `blocked` with explicit message |
   660	| TC-4.3b | self-review prompt evolves by pass | `prompt-assembly.test.ts` | self-review fixtures | pass-specific snippets differ |
   661	| TC-4.4a | clear local fixes can be applied automatically | `story-implement-command.test.ts` | provider returns noncontroversial fix list | result records auto-applied fixes |
   662	| TC-4.4b | uncertain fix is surfaced | `story-implement-command.test.ts` | provider flags uncertainty | result outcome `needs-human-ruling` or surfaced finding |
   663	| TC-4.5a | implementor result has full contract | `result-contracts.test.ts` | valid result object | schema accepts required fields only |
   664	
   665	## Flow 3: Story Verification and Quick Fix
   666	
   667	**Covers:** AC-3.3, AC-3.4b, AC-3.4c, AC-5.1 to AC-5.5
   668	
   669	`story-verify` launches fresh verifiers by default. They should not reuse prior verifier sessions. `quick-fix` is intentionally lighter than a full implementor pass. It exists for small, bounded corrections that do not justify re-running the full story implementation workflow.
   670	
   671	The orchestration rule here is simple. The CLI can execute verification or a quick fix, but it cannot decide that a story is accepted. It only returns disciplined findings and outcomes. The orchestrator still interprets them and runs the final gate.
   672	
   673	Re-verification is proportional to the substance of the fix. Substantial fixes should trigger fresh verifier sessions. Small, nit, or cleanup-only fixes may be spot-checked by the orchestrator with a local gate rerun and no re-verification dispatch.
   674	
   675	```mermaid
   676	sequenceDiagram
   677	    participant Orchestrator
   678	    participant CLI
   679	    participant Adapter1 as Verifier Adapter A
   680	    participant Adapter2 as Verifier Adapter B
   681	    participant QuickFix as Quick Fix Adapter
   682	
   683	    Note over Orchestrator,QuickFix: AC-5.1 to AC-5.5
   684	    Orchestrator->>CLI: story-verify --story-id <id>
   685	    CLI->>Adapter1: launch verifier 1
   686	    CLI->>Adapter2: launch verifier 2
   687	    Adapter1-->>CLI: verifier result
   688	    Adapter2-->>CLI: verifier result
   689	    CLI-->>Orchestrator: verifier batch
   690	    Orchestrator->>CLI: quick-fix --request ...
   691	    CLI->>QuickFix: run bounded correction
   692	    QuickFix-->>CLI: quick-fix result
   693	    CLI-->>Orchestrator: quick-fix result
   694	```
   695	
   696	### Story Verifier Result Contract
   697	
   698	```typescript
   699	export interface StoryVerifierResult {
   700	  resultId: string;
   701	  outcome: "pass" | "revise" | "block";
   702	  verifierLabel: string;
   703	  provider: "claude-code" | "codex" | "copilot";
   704	  model: string;
   705	  story: {
   706	    id: string;
   707	    title: string;
   708	  };
   709	  artifactsRead: string[];
   710	  reviewScopeSummary: string;
   711	  findings: VerifierFinding[];
   712	  requirementCoverage: {
   713	    verified: string[];
   714	    unverified: string[];
   715	  };
   716	  gatesRun: Array<{ command: string; result: "pass" | "fail" | "not-run" }>;
   717	  mockOrShimAuditFindings: string[];
   718	  recommendedFixScope:
   719	    | "same-session-implementor"
   720	    | "quick-fix"
   950	## Verification Gate Resolution
   951	
   952	The runtime must not guess gate commands ad hoc.
   953	
   954	1. explicit command flags
   955	2. explicit run-config values, if later added
   956	3. discovered package scripts
   957	4. supporting policy docs or CI config
   958	5. otherwise `needs-user-decision`
   959	
   960	```typescript
   961	export interface VerificationGateConfig {
   962	  storyGate: string;
   963	  epicGate: string;
   964	  source: "cli-flag" | "run-config" | "package-scripts" | "policy-doc" | "ci-config";
   965	}
   966	```
   967	
   968	The preflight result should surface this config so the orchestrator can persist it into `team-impl-log.md`.
   969	
   970	### Canonical Gate Mapping
   971	
   972	Until a project overrides the defaults, the expected mapping for this repo is:
   973	
   974	| Gate | Command |
   975	|---|---|
   976	| Story gate | `bun run green-verify` |
   977	| Epic gate | `bun run verify-all` |
   978	
   979	This mapping should appear consistently in:
   980	
   981	- `preflight` output
   982	- `team-impl-log.md`
   983	- prompt assembly inputs
   984	- process-playbook examples
   985	
   986	## Packaging and Build Integration
   987	
   988	The source runtime stays modular. The packaged skill should contain one bundled Node-compatible JS file.
   989	
   990	### Build Steps

156	### Result Routing Matrix
   157	
   158	This matrix is authoritative for handler implementation and tests.
   159	
   160	| Command Outcome | Envelope Status | Exit Code | Orchestrator Action |
   161	|---|---|---:|---|
   162	| `ready` | `ok` | 0 | proceed to next setup step |
   163	| `ready-for-verification` | `ok` | 0 | run verification or next bounded step |
   164	| `pass` | `ok` | 0 | evaluate for acceptance or next stage |
   165	| `cleaned` | `ok` | 0 | move into epic verification |
   166	| `ready-for-closeout` | `ok` | 0 | run final orchestrator-owned gate |
   167	| `needs-user-decision` | `needs-user-decision` | 2 | pause and request human clarification |
   168	| `needs-followup-fix` | `ok` | 2 | route same-session follow-up or fix path |
   169	| `needs-human-ruling` | `needs-user-decision` | 2 | pause for orchestrator/human ruling |
   170	| `revise` | `ok` | 2 | route fix and rerun verification |
   171	| `needs-more-routing` | `ok` | 2 | select another bounded correction path |
   172	| `needs-more-cleanup` | `ok` | 2 | continue cleanup cycle |
   173	| `needs-fixes` | `ok` | 2 | route fixes before closeout |
   174	| `needs-more-verification` | `ok` | 2 | launch additional verification/synthesis |
   175	| `blocked` | `blocked` | 3 | stop and inspect blocking error details |
   176	| parse/schema/runtime failure | `error` | 1 | treat as CLI failure rather than feature-state outcome |
   760	| TC-5.1a | default verifier count is two | `story-verify-command.test.ts` | no override config | two verifier launches requested |
   761	| TC-5.1b | re-verification uses fresh sessions | `provider-adapter.test.ts` | prior verifier session ids | new verifier sessions used |
   762	| TC-5.2a | verifier contract complete | `result-contracts.test.ts` | sample verifier result | schema requires identity, findings, coverage, gates |
   763	| TC-5.2b | additional observations retained | `story-verify-command.test.ts` | provider returns soft observations | result includes additional observations |
   764	| TC-5.2c | mock/shim audit required | `story-verify-command.test.ts` | integration-facing fixture | mock audit field included and populated when needed |
   765	| TC-5.3a | quick-fix routing supported | `quick-fix-command.test.ts` | bounded fix request | quick-fix operation executes without full implementor path |
   766	| TC-5.3b | quick-fix envelope stays explicit while payload stays unstructured | `result-contracts.test.ts` | quick-fix result fixture | validate only the outer envelope for quick-fix outputs |
   767	| TC-5.4a | implementor uncertainty preserved for orchestrator | `story-implement-command.test.ts` | uncertain implementor output | surfaced in result |
   768	| TC-5.4b | verifier disagreement preserved | `story-verify-command.test.ts` | conflicting verifier results | batch result indicates revise/block instead of silent resolution |
   769	| TC-5.5a | final story gate not owned by CLI | `cli-operations-doc.test.ts` | command guide fixture | public docs state gate is orchestrator-owned |
   770	
   771	## Flow 4: Epic Cleanup, Verification, and Synthesis
   772	
   773	**Covers:** AC-7.1 to AC-7.3, AC-8.1 to AC-8.4
   774	
   775	At epic closeout the CLI executes three different kinds of work: cleanup-only correction, epic-level verifier batches, and synthesis. The orchestrator still determines whether the run can close. The CLI should never blur these responsibilities into one vague “finish the epic” command.
   776	
   777	The cleanup operation is distinct because it acts on an orchestrator-curated cleanup batch. It is not a general-purpose retry. It is a bounded correction-only workflow designed to start epic verification from the cleaned state rather than from an accumulation of known-but-deferred issues.
   778	
   779	```mermaid
   780	sequenceDiagram
   781	    participant Orchestrator
   782	    participant CLI
   783	    participant Cleanup as Cleanup Runner
   784	    participant EV as Epic Verifier Batch
   785	    participant SYN as Epic Synthesizer
   786	
   787	    Note over Orchestrator,SYN: AC-7.1 to AC-8.4
   788	    Orchestrator->>CLI: epic-cleanup --batch <cleanup.md>
   789	    CLI->>Cleanup: apply approved cleanup items
   790	    Cleanup-->>CLI: cleanup result
   791	    CLI-->>Orchestrator: cleanup outcome
   792	    Orchestrator->>CLI: epic-verify
   793	    CLI->>EV: launch epic verifiers
   794	    EV-->>CLI: epic verifier results
   795	    CLI-->>Orchestrator: verifier batch
   796	    Orchestrator->>CLI: epic-synthesize
   797	    CLI->>SYN: verify and consolidate findings
   798	    SYN-->>CLI: synthesis result
   799	    CLI-->>Orchestrator: synthesis outcome
   800	```
   801	
   802	### Epic Verifier Result
   803	
   804	```typescript
   805	export interface EpicVerifierResult {
   806	  resultId: string;
   807	  outcome: "pass" | "revise" | "block";
   808	  provider: "claude-code" | "codex" | "copilot";
   809	  model: string;
   810	  reviewerLabel: string;
   811	  crossStoryFindings: string[];
   812	  architectureFindings: string[];
   813	  epicCoverageAssessment: string[];
   814	  mockOrShimAuditFindings: string[];
   815	  blockingFindings: VerifierFinding[];
   816	  nonBlockingFindings: VerifierFinding[];
   817	  unresolvedItems: string[];
   818	  gateResult: "pass" | "fail" | "not-run";
   819	}
   820	```

## Summary
Implemented all 15 scoped items in the `ls-claude-impl` fix pass, including the 2 blocking runtime fixes, the 2 contract drifts, the 6 minor improvements, and the 5 spec/doc alignment updates. `bun test`, `bun run green-verify`, and `bun run verify-all` all pass; `verify-all` still shows the expected visible `smoke:impl-cli skipped` notice.

- ID: `B2` | Outcome: `implemented` | Files Changed: `processes/impl-cli/core/provider-checks.ts`, `processes/impl-cli/core/result-contracts.ts`, `processes/impl-cli/commands/preflight.ts` | Tests Added/Modified/Removed: `preflight-command.test.ts` added 4-tier cases; `provider-adapter.test.ts` updated tier expectations

- ID: `M2` | Outcome: `implemented` | Files Changed: `processes/impl-cli/commands/preflight.ts` | Tests Added/Modified/Removed: `preflight-command.test.ts` added malformed-config artifact persistence coverage
- ID: `m1` | Outcome: `implemented` | Files Changed: `processes/impl-cli/core/epic-cleanup.ts` | Tests Added/Modified/Removed: `epic-cleanup-command.test.ts` added negated/superseded `APPROVED` no-op coverage
